identity.name: League
identity.description: League provides AI-powered healthcare consumer experience platforms to payers, providers, and healthcare organizations to improve health engagement and outcomes.

team.ceo_background: Serial entrepreneur who previously founded and sold Kobo to Rakuten for $315M USD and DocSpace in 2000. Co-founded League in 2014 with three friends.

competitive.market_position: Leading AI-first healthcare CX platform with 63M+ contracted users globally

direct_competitors.name: Nayya
direct_competitors.differentiator: Focuses on AI benefits guidance and decision automation for employees

direct_competitors.name: Elevate
direct_competitors.differentiator: Specializes in AI-powered benefits administration software for employers

direct_competitors.name: Heka
direct_competitors.differentiator: Emphasizes personalized employee benefits and wellness programs

direct_competitors.name: Euphoric
direct_competitors.differentiator: Positions as global benefits administration platform for multinational employers

tailwinds: Growing demand for AI-driven healthcare personalization
headwinds: Competition from larger tech players entering healthcare

news.summary: League demonstrates strong momentum with significant user growth milestones and continuous AI platform innovation. The company has achieved 63 million contracted users (271% YoY growth) while launching advanced AI capabilities including Agent Teams and Care Gap closure solutions. Recent partnerships with major healthcare brands and expansion into new markets signal strong market traction.

recent_events.event: Announced Spring '26 Release featuring orchestrated AI care navigation and Care Gap Agent Team at annual Connect conference
Launched League AI with Agent Teams multi-agent system and announced partnership with Arcadia for longitudinal patient data integration
Reached 40 million contracted users milestone (160% YoY growth) and secured $100M USD credit facility from RBCx
Unveiled League Agent Teams at HLTH 2025, announcing partnership with Australia's Medibank and new AI-powered features

moat_signals: 63M+ contracted user base creating network effects
Decade of healthcare consumer experience data

nodes.name: Nayya
nodes.relationship: Direct competitor in AI-powered healthcare benefits navigation

nodes.name: Elevate
nodes.relationship: Competitor in AI-powered benefits administration space

nodes.name: Arcadia
nodes.relationship: Strategic data partnership for longitudinal patient records integration

nodes.name: AWS
nodes.relationship: Cloud infrastructure and AI services partnership

fit_score.rationale: League represents a compelling fit with Sagard's healthcare thesis as a leading AI-first digital health platform with proven scale and enterprise traction. The company's B2B SaaS model serving major healthcare payers aligns well with Sagard's preference for scalable healthcare technology. With 63M+ users, $285M raised, and clear path to profitability by 2026, League demonstrates the growth-stage maturity suitable for VC investment.

confidence.note: Strong coverage across identity, funding, and competitive landscape with consistent data from multiple sources, though team information was limited to founder/CEO details only.
